transcript: Yeah, hi. I’m, ah, Reese Baptiste; I’m new in town, here, and I was lookin’ for a new, ah, fishin’ store.
So you guys are close to me, here.
So I need to get a new fishin’ pole. Last place I went to sold me this short one, you know? Whenever I reeled it in I had fish flippin’ like wild off my hook!—

—Oh, they were spinnin’ like wildfire on there. And then I went back there and he sold me a rod that was so long I actually hooked some owl out of a tree; it was bizzare! Real bizarre! So I need something that’s gonna do the job, is what I’m tryin’ to say.
Well, the only way I can figure that out is where you’re fishing and a—and whereabouts you’re going.
Oh, I wanted to do some brackish fishing, primarily.
That’s not answering my question—is whether you’re going fishing on a pier, a bulkhead, or a beach?

H—hey, guy! I’m up at two, two-thirty in the mornin’, sortin’ my worms, you know? I got hookworms, roundworms, ribbon worms; I got worms goin’ wild in my—in my toolshed! So I just need somethin’ that’s gonna reduce jigging; I don’t got time for backlash on my spool! Does that answer your question?
No! What I’m looking for is whether you’re fishing on the beach, on a pier, or a bulkhead, or are you—

—On a pier. Are you freshwater or saltwater? You could be doing striped bass fishing, which is saltwater, or you could do freshwater fishing, which is, uh, largemouth bass, uh, crappies, depending on what you’re looking for. I—I don’t know where you’re gonna fish at; that’s why I’m asking you where are you going.
So you’re fishin’ the river; so you’re lookin’ for somethin’ about eight- or nine-foot fishin’ pole.
Okay. Whatever you recommend, I mean.
Okay. Well, only thing I can tell you to do is stop on by and we’ll go from there.
I wanna look into maybe usin’ some buzzbait or bucktail—
Somethin’ where I can do bottom bouncing under my boat, there.
Well, once again, you have to come down so we can figure it out. I can’t do anything on the phone ‘cause you can’t see it.
[Voicemail of Hook House Bait and Tackle enters call]
Hello, this is Hook House Bait and Tackle. Monday through Thursday, we’re open 6 A.M. to 8 P.M.—
[Hook House Bait and Tackle exits call]
Yeah, why don’t ya tell me?
What’s the bag limit around here, anyhow—
—What was that in the background that I just heard?
I thought that was on your end.
No, that was on your end.
Hey, I’m the only one here, guy.
[Steve 2, an employee of Atlantic Bait and Tackle, enters call]
Atlantic Bait. Wow. Who’s this, Pete?
I don’t know who’s doing this. Somebody’s playing games here.
Hey, guy, I’m sorry; we keep getting cut off here. I’m just tryin’ to figure out what’s goin’ on.

Yeah, well, I keep—I keep—I keep getting hooked between you and another bait and tackle store. I don’t know who you’re trying to call. But I don’t understand this, and I’m not gonna deal with it any longer. So, at this point, that’s twice that’s happened, and, uh, other b—tackle store are answerin’ in the background. Either you’re callin’ more than one place at one time or this is a complete scam. Because, all of a sudden, there was three people on the phone from—one from Atlantic Bait and somebody else. W—what—who are you and what do you want?
Okay, guy. What time do you open up ‘ere—
—is the last thing I need to find out.
Okay, and if I wanna pick up a shoal, for instance, can I get a downrigger from ya, or a fish finder?
Hey, Fisherman’s Supply, can you hear me?
Now I—I don’t know what this is, but they—this guy that’s calling me is calling you at the same time and this is ridiculous. I mean, I’m not trying to, uh—I—I did not call you; somebody else did—

—They called me and they called you at the same time. I don’t know if you’ve got the same phone number coming through your phone numerous times, but I just did. Uh, you know, I apologize for this. I’m a bait and tackle store up north and I know that—who you guys are. Fisherman’s Supply is very popular. And I apologize, but I’m not the one doing this.
—Yeah, I don’t know what’s goin’ on—
I was lookin’ for some, ah, barbs, or what kind you would recommend.
What kinda barbs are you lookin’ for?
Well, that’s what I’m tryin’ to find out, what kind ya got and how much they are.
What are you trying to do with it?
I’m tryin’ to fish, here, first thing tomorrow mornin’—
[Pete, an employee of Atlantic Bait and Tackle, enters call]
I don’t know; I can’t see ya! Gimme three guesses! Who’s this?
Ah, I don’t know—who—who’s on the phone right now? ‘Cause some guy keeps playing games with my phone, over here, and I’m gettin’ a little crazy—
—Nah, this is Atlantic Bait and Tackle; you need bait? I got bait.
Hey, Pete! How ya doin’, Pete? This is, ah, Steve; I’m from Up—
—Hey, listen: I’m from Up Front Bait and Tackle. I did not call this number; somebody else called this number—
—and then connected the two of us.
He—hey, Pete, have a good day, man. Sorry ‘bout that.
Yeah, you too. Bye now. Right.
[sfx] Yeah, Up Front Bait? Can ya hear me?
[Woman, an employee of a bait and tackle store, is present in call]
Yes, hello. How can I help you?
This is the bait and tackle store.
You know, I—I—I’m sorry to say this, but I—I—somebody’s playin’ around with our phones. They’ve been—
—calling me and callin’ somebody else together, and the two of us—for some strange reason, they’re hanging up—
—And we’re getting connected. I don’t know what’s goin’ on. I—
—It’s drivin’ me crazy! I don’t know if you’re—
—gettin’ the same thing over and over and over and over and over and over again.
—So, the gentleman said that somebody was gonna go on the phone that needed to buy some bait or some tackle and you came on.
—I—I’m sorry, th—I—they’re doin’ the same things to me. I’m a bait and tackle store, myself. Okay, um—
—and this—this is what they’re doin: they’re playin’ games with the two of us and they’ve been doing it, now, for about a half an hour. So every time—
—the guy calls me—he calls me first, then he calls another store and then when we pick up he hangs up; it’s drivin’ me crazy—
—So I’m just lettin’ you know what’s goin’ on; I don’t want you to think it was me callin’ you. But this is—this—this is—
—about the sixth phone call in a row that I’ve gotten.
Well, I’m glad that we straightened that out.
I just don’t wanna see it keep happening to you, too, as well. ‘Cause this would really be annoying to both of us. [unintelligible]—
—Because the c—the number comes up “unavailable”, so they’re blocking it—
—Right, and it’s comin’ over here as a private number, yes—
[Man 2, an employee of Tight Lines Fly Fishing, enters call]
—And I tried calling it back and it won’t go through.
—Alright. Now we got a third one. I don’t know if you heard ‘em. Can you hear him in the background?—
—Hello? Hi, this is Tight Lines Fly Fishing—
See? Now they did it again; they got three of us connected. Do you hear that?
—[sfx] Yeah, hello? I’m lookin’ for nightcrawlers up here.
I’m sorry, I don’t have any more—
[Voicemail (?) of Captain Andy of RipTide Bait and Tackle enters cal]
—Hey, this is Captain Andy. RipTide Bait and Tackle—
Yeah, I—I—I got you now. This is Tight Lines Fly Fishing. We don’t have any live bait at all. I’m sorry.
[Voicemail of Captain Eric of Saltwater Therapy Charters enters call]
Hi, this is Captain Eric with Saltwater Therapy Charters. I’m out fishin’ right now. I’ll give you a call when I return. Have a great day.
